summary: Added a dev-server proxy entry for `/ppt-template-assets` in `rspack/rspack.dev.js`, forwarding to `http://localhost:8899` with `changeOrigin: true`.
implementation: Inserted a new object in the existing `devServer.proxy` array next to other static/proxy routes.
risks: Proxy target assumes the asset service is running locally on port 8899.

summary: Implemented template preview modal in Library page using Ant Design Modal and Carousel with arrows/infinite disabled.
implementation: Added preview state and TemplatePreview data, converted template preview links/buttons to open a controlled Modal, added a responsive-height Carousel (`arrows` and `infinite={false}`) with image-backed slide previews and overlay text. Featured card arrow also opens its preview.
tests: Ran `npm run lint`; current repo has pre-existing TS errors in `src/store/PptWorkflowStore.ts` and `src/store/UserStore.ts`, not related to this change. Started dev server, Rspack compiled successfully, opened `/library` in the in-app browser, and verified the modal DOM contains `prev`/`next` carousel buttons and slide content. `git diff --check -- src/view/Library/index.tsx` passed with only the existing LF/CRLF warning.
risks: Carousel slide images currently reuse existing library/stock assets as preview placeholders; replace with real per-slide template screenshots when backend/template asset URLs are available.

summary: Reimplemented Library template list UI using the same templates API as createPpt and restored the requested preview modal/carousel behavior.
implementation: Imported `getTemplates`/`PptTemplate` from `@/api/ppt`; added loading/templates/preview state; Library now fetches `/aiPpt/templates`, renders the first template as the featured card and the rest as standard cards matching the existing static visual style, with fallback static templates for empty/failing data. Preview opens an Ant Design `Modal` containing `Carousel arrows infinite={false}` with 4 placeholder preview slides built from the template cover plus existing stock images. Added URL resolution matching createPpt's base API handling.
tests: `npm run lint` is blocked by unrelated existing syntax errors in `src/components/SlideEditor/layout/types.ts`. Opened `http://localhost:6001/library` in the in-app browser using the existing dev server; verified real template API data rendered (20 preview buttons) and clicking a preview opened a modal with `prev`/`next` carousel arrows and Slide 1-4 content. `git diff --check -- src/view/Library/index.tsx` passed with only LF/CRLF warning.
risks: Preview slides currently use placeholder cover/stock images until the real template-page data/source is provided. Category/search controls remain visual only, consistent with the existing static page.

summary: Updated Library template preview carousel to use template `pages` SVG URLs from the templates API.
implementation: Added optional `pages?: string[]` to `PptTemplate`. In Library, `previewSlides` now prefers `previewTemplate.pages` and maps each page URL to a carousel slide rendered as an object-contain SVG/image without overlay text. `resolveAssetUrl` now preserves `/ppt-template-assets/...` paths so they hit the dedicated dev proxy instead of being prefixed by `/dev-api`. Falls back to cover/stock placeholder slides when `pages` is absent.
tests: Verified in the in-app browser on `http://localhost:6001/library`: real template data rendered 20 preview buttons; opening a preview produced modal image sources starting with `/ppt-template-assets/...`. `git diff --check -- src/api/ppt.ts src/view/Library/index.tsx` passed with only LF/CRLF warnings.
risks: Only the currently active carousel slide image is guaranteed present in DOM at once due to antd carousel rendering behavior; this is expected. Full `npm run lint` remains blocked by unrelated existing errors in `src/components/SlideEditor/layout/types.ts` from earlier verification.

summary: Added a new template authoring page with API wrappers, route, navigation entry, canvas crop/upload area, SlideEditor-based SVG editor, resource panels, and dev proxy support for template assets/resources.
implementation: Created `templateAuthoring` API module for upload, resource lists, project create/analyze/generate/save/validate/publish. Added `/template-authoring` route and sidebar/top title. Built `TemplateAuthoring` page with current project state, source image upload, canvas-backed crop rectangle/upload flow, generate template flow that calls analyze then generateDraft, SlideEditor reuse for SVG editing, save/validate/publish actions, resource panels for SVG/bitmap/recent assets, preview modal, and resource insertion as `<image href=...>` without base64. Added dev proxies for `/ppt-template-assets` and `/ppt-template-resources`.
tests: `npm run build:qa` passed; Rspack compiled with existing asset size warnings. `npm run lint` remains blocked by unrelated pre-existing syntax errors in `src/components/SlideEditor/layout/types.ts`. In-app browser hit `/template-authoring` but was redirected to login by existing AuthGuard, so runtime route access requires login.
risks: Page assumes generateDraft returns an SVG string in `data.pages[0]`, `data.svg`, or `data.pageSvg`; may need adjustment if backend returns page objects. Resource list parser supports array/list/items/records. Canvas crop uploads PNG; source image must be same-origin/proxied for canvas extraction. New `PPT_TEMPLATE_AUTHORING_API_GUIDE.md` is untracked and appears user-provided, left untouched.
